_comment: DO NOT EDIT: FILE GENERATED AUTOMATICALLY BY PANEL
name: BeamMP Server
author: filipnone@pelican.dev

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# ──────────────────────────────────────
# BeamMP Server Installation Script
# ──────────────────────────────────────

mkdir -p /mnt/server
cd /mnt/server

# Remove any old binary
rm -f BeamMP-Server

# ── Detect Architecture ──
ARCH=$(uname -m)
case "$ARCH" in
    x86_64)  ARCH="x86_64" ;;
    aarch64) ARCH="arm64" ;;
    arm64)   ARCH="arm64" ;;
    *)
        echo "ERROR: Unsupported architecture: $ARCH"
        exit 1
        ;;
esac
echo "Detected architecture: $ARCH"

# ── Detect OS / Distro ──
if [ -f /etc/os-release ]; then
    . /etc/os-release
    DISTRO_ID=$(echo "$ID" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
    VERSION_MAJOR=$(echo "$VERSION_ID" | cut -d. -f1)
else
    DISTRO_ID="debian"
    VERSION_MAJOR="12"
fi
echo "Detected distro: $DISTRO_ID $VERSION_MAJOR"

# ── Map distro to BeamMP build name ──
# BeamMP provides: debian.12, debian.13, ubuntu.22.04, ubuntu.24.04
# Older releases also had debian.11
case "$DISTRO_ID" in
    ubuntu)
        case "$VERSION_MAJOR" in
            24) BUILD_OS="ubuntu.24.04" ;;
            22) BUILD_OS="ubuntu.22.04" ;;
            20) BUILD_OS="ubuntu.22.04" ;;  # closest match
            *)  BUILD_OS="ubuntu.24.04" ;;   # default to latest
        esac
        ;;
    debian|*)
        case "$VERSION_MAJOR" in
            13) BUILD_OS="debian.13" ;;
            12) BUILD_OS="debian.12" ;;
            11) BUILD_OS="debian.12" ;;      # 11 builds dropped in newer releases, use 12
            *)  BUILD_OS="debian.12" ;;
        esac
        ;;
esac

# ── Allow user override ──
if [ -n "${BEAMMP_BUILD:-}" ]; then
    BUILD_OS="${BEAMMP_BUILD}"
    echo "Using user-specified build: $BUILD_OS"
fi

TARGET_NAME="BeamMP-Server.${BUILD_OS}.${ARCH}"
echo "Target asset: $TARGET_NAME"

# ── Fetch release data ──
echo "Fetching GitHub releases..."
LATEST_JSON=$(curl --silent --connect-timeout 15 --retry 3 \
    "https://api.github.com/repos/BeamMP/BeamMP-Server/releases/latest")

if [ -z "$LATEST_JSON" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to fetch release data from GitHub API"
    exit 1
fi

# ── Determine version ──
if [ -z "${VERSION:-}" ] || [ "${VERSION}" = "latest" ]; then
    echo "Using latest version..."
    RELEASE_DATA="$LATEST_JSON"
else
    echo "Looking for version: ${VERSION}..."
    ALL_RELEASES=$(curl --silent --connect-timeout 15 --retry 3 \
        "https://api.github.com/repos/BeamMP/BeamMP-Server/releases?per_page=50")

    # Try exact tag match first
    RELEASE_DATA=$(echo "$ALL_RELEASES" | jq -r --arg TAG "${VERSION}" '.[] | select(.tag_name == $TAG)' | head -1)

    # Fall back to partial match
    if [ -z "$RELEASE_DATA" ] || [ "$RELEASE_DATA" = "null" ]; then
        echo "Exact tag '${VERSION}' not found, trying partial match..."
        RELEASE_DATA=$(echo "$ALL_RELEASES" | jq -r --arg TAG "${VERSION}" '.[] | select(.tag_name | contains($TAG))' | head -1)
    fi

    # Fall back to latest
    if [ -z "$RELEASE_DATA" ] || [ "$RELEASE_DATA" = "null" ]; then
        echo "Version '${VERSION}' not found, falling back to latest..."
        RELEASE_DATA="$LATEST_JSON"
    fi
fi

RELEASE_TAG=$(echo "$RELEASE_DATA" | jq -r '.tag_name // "unknown"')
echo "Using release: $RELEASE_TAG"

# ── Find matching asset ──
# Strategy 1: Exact match
DOWNLOAD_URL=$(echo "$RELEASE_DATA" | jq -r --arg NAME "$TARGET_NAME" \
    '.assets[]? | select(.name == $NAME) | .browser_download_url' | head -1)

# Strategy 2: Same distro, any version
if [ -z "$DOWNLOAD_URL" ] || [ "$DOWNLOAD_URL" = "null" ]; then
    echo "Exact match not found, trying same distro pattern..."
    DISTRO_BASE=$(echo "$BUILD_OS" | cut -d. -f1)
    DOWNLOAD_URL=$(echo "$RELEASE_DATA" | jq -r --arg DISTRO "$DISTRO_BASE" --arg ARCH "$ARCH" \
        '.assets[]? | select(.name | test("^BeamMP-Server\\." + $DISTRO + "\\..*\\." + $ARCH + "$")) | .browser_download_url' | head -1)
fi

# Strategy 3: Any Linux build for this arch
if [ -z "$DOWNLOAD_URL" ] || [ "$DOWNLOAD_URL" = "null" ]; then
    echo "Still not found, trying any Linux $ARCH build..."
    DOWNLOAD_URL=$(echo "$RELEASE_DATA" | jq -r --arg ARCH "$ARCH" \
        '.assets[]? | select(.name | test("^BeamMP-Server\\..*\\." + $ARCH + "$")) | .browser_download_url' | head -1)
fi

# ── Final check ──
if [ -z "$DOWNLOAD_URL" ] || [ "$DOWNLOAD_URL" = "null" ]; then
    echo "============================================"
    echo "ERROR: Could not find a suitable BeamMP-Server binary!"
    echo "  Build requested: $TARGET_NAME"
    echo "  Release: $RELEASE_TAG"
    echo "  Available assets:"
    echo "$RELEASE_DATA" | jq -r '.assets[]? | "    \(.name)"'
    echo ""
    echo "  Try setting BEAMMP_BUILD to one of: debian.12, debian.13, ubuntu.22.04, ubuntu.24.04"
    echo "============================================"
    exit 1
fi

echo "Downloading: $DOWNLOAD_URL"
curl -sSL --connect-timeout 30 --retry 3 -o BeamMP-Server "$DOWNLOAD_URL"

# ── Verify download ──
if [ ! -f BeamMP-Server ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Download failed - BeamMP-Server file not created"
    exit 1
fi

FILE_SIZE=$(stat -c%s BeamMP-Server 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z BeamMP-Server 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
if [ "$FILE_SIZE" -lt 100000 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Downloaded file is too small ($FILE_SIZE bytes). Something went wrong."
    cat BeamMP-Server 2>/dev/null || true
    exit 1
fi

chmod +x BeamMP-Server
echo "BeamMP-Server downloaded successfully ($FILE_SIZE bytes)"

# ── Create default ServerConfig.toml if missing ──
if [ ! -f ServerConfig.toml ]; then
    cat > ServerConfig.toml << 'TOML_EOF'
# This is the BeamMP-Server config file.
# Help & Documentation: https://wiki.beammp.com/en/home/server-maintenance
# IMPORTANT: Fill in the AuthKey with the key from https://keymaster.beammp.com/

[General]
Name = 'BeamMP Server'
Port = 30814
AuthKey = ''
LogChat = true
Tags = 'Freeroam'
Debug = false
Private = true
MaxCars = 1
MaxPlayers = 8
Map = '/levels/gridmap_v2/info.json'
Description = 'BeamMP Default Description'
ResourceFolder = 'Resources'

[Misc]
ImScaredOfUpdates = false
SendErrorsShowMessage = true
SendErrors = true
TOML_EOF
    echo "Default ServerConfig.toml created."
else
    echo "Existing ServerConfig.toml found, preserving it."
fi

echo "-----------------------------------------"
echo "BeamMP Server installation complete!"
echo "Release: $RELEASE_TAG  |  Build: $BUILD_OS  |  Arch: $ARCH"
echo "-----------------------------------------"

variables.name: BeamMP Server Version
variables.description: BeamMP server version to install. Invalid values fall back to latest.

variables.name: Authentication Key
variables.description: The authentication key for your server. Obtain one from the BeamMP Keymaster: https://keymaster.beammp.com/. REQUIRED for the server to run.

variables.name: Server Name
variables.description: The name of your BeamMP server as shown on the public server list.

variables.name: Server Description
variables.description: A short description shown on the server list.

variables.name: Max Players
variables.description: Maximum number of players allowed on the server simultaneously.

variables.name: Max Cars
variables.description: Maximum number of cars each player can spawn.

variables.name: Private
variables.description: If 'true', the server won't appear on the public server list. Players must join via direct IP.

variables.name: Map
variables.description: Map selection path: To specify the active map, update the MAPNAME variable within the directory path /levels/MAPNAME/info.json

variables.name: Chat Logging
variables.description: Log chat messages to the server console/log.

variables.name: Tags
variables.description: Comma-separated tags to help players find your server. Example: 'Freeroam,Racing,Roleplay'

variables.name: Debug Mode
variables.description: Enable debug logging. Turn off for production servers (may impact performance).

variables.name: Resource Folder
variables.description: Path to the Resources folder for Lua plugins and server assets.

variables.name: Hide Update Messages
variables.description: Suppress periodic 'new version available' messages. It's recommended to keep this false and always update.

variables.name: Send Error Reports
variables.description: Send crash/error reports to BeamMP developers. This helps them fix bugs faster and has no performance impact.

variables.name: Show Send Errors Message
variables.description: Show the 'SendErrors' notice on server startup.

variables.name: Build Override
variables.description: Force a specific BeamMP build type. Leave blank for auto-detection. Valid values: debian.12, debian.13, ubuntu.22.04, ubuntu.24.04. Only change this if auto-detection picks the wrong binary.
